_note: STAGING FILE — Do not move to Felix inbox until metrc-phase1.json has completed successfully. All Phase 1 infrastructure (migrations 042-044, src/lib/metrc-csv/ module, route stubs) must exist before these tasks run.

tasks.name: METRC CSV 3A: Planting family #2 #17 #18 #22
tasks.instructions:
Read CLAUDE.md and docs/metrc-csv-design/integration-proposal.md before starting.

PREREQUISITE CHECK: Verify that src/lib/metrc-csv/index.ts, src/lib/metrc-csv/writer.ts, src/lib/metrc-csv/types.ts, and src/api/routes/metrc-csv.ts all exist. If any are missing, STOP and report that Phase 1 infrastructure is missing.

CONTEXT: This is Phase 3A of the METRC CSV Upload Service. Phase 1 has already built the core infrastructure (CSV writer, module scaffold, migrations 042-044 with all new tables, reference data, and column extensions on existing tables). Study the existing generator files src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/additive-template.ts and src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/plants-waste.ts as implementation patterns before writing any code.

TASK: Implement four upload types in the 'planting family' — all create plant batch records in METRC.

The generator pattern for all types:
1. Accept typed params (define interface in the generator file)
2. Validate inputs (route handler uses Zod; generator trusts the validated input)
3. Open a db.transaction(() => { ... }) that: reads needed data, builds CSV rows array, writes CSV via the shared CsvWriter utility, applies all side effects to Cultivate DB, inserts cv_metrc_csv_uploads row
4. After transaction, rename .tmp to final path
5. Return { csv_file_path, row_count, upload_id, warnings }

All generators go in src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/. All route handlers are added to the existing src/api/routes/metrc-csv.ts file.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #2: Create Plantings ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/create-plantings.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/create-plantings
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/CreatePlantings_template.csv
Exact headers: Name,Type,Count,Strain,Location,Sublocation,PatientLicenseNumber,ActualDate,IngredientPlantBatchName

Request body (Zod schema in route handler):
  name: string (the METRC batch name, e.g. 'AK-47 Clone 5/26/2026')
  type: string (from cv_metrc_plant_types — 'Clone' or 'Seed')
  count: number (integer, positive)
  strain: string (must match cv_strains.name)
  location: string (must match cv_locations.metrc_name)
  sublocation: string | null
  patient_license_number: string | null
  actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  ingredient_batch_names: string[] (1+ names of source batches; each produces one CSV row with same Name/Type/Count/Strain/Location but different IngredientPlantBatchName)
  source_type: 'none' | 'package' | 'plant' | 'ingredient_batches'
  source_package_label: string | null (24-char, when source_type='package')
  source_plant_label: string | null (24-char, when source_type='plant')

CSV output: one row per ingredient_batch_name. If ingredient_batch_names is empty, produce one row with IngredientPlantBatchName empty.

DB side effects (within transaction):
  - INSERT INTO cv_batches with status='germ', metrc_source_type=source_type, metrc_source_batch_name (if applicable), metrc_ingredient_batch_names=JSON(ingredient_batch_names), plant_count_initial=count, plant_count_current=count, sow_date=actual_date
  - The batch_id of the newly inserted cv_batches row is returned in response as batch_id

Phase 5 API: POST /plantbatches/v2/plantings — returns { Ids: [...] } for newly created batches.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #17: Plantings from Package ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/plantings-from-package.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/plantings-from-package
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PlantingsFromPackages_template.csv
Exact headers: PackageLabel,PackageAdjustmentAmount,PackageAdjustmentUnitOfMeasureName,PlantBatchName,PlantBatchType,PlantCount,StrainName,LocationName,SublocationName,PatientLicenseNumber,PlantedDate,UnpackagedDate

Request body:
  package_label: string (24-char; FK to cv_metrc_packages.package_tag)
  package_adjustment_amount: number | null (quantity removed from package to create this planting)
  package_adjustment_uom: string | null (UoM for adjustment)
  plant_batch_name: string
  plant_batch_type: string ('Clone' or 'Seed')
  plant_count: number (integer, positive)
  strain_name: string
  location_name: string (metrc_name from cv_locations)
  sublocation_name: string | null
  patient_license_number: string | null
  planted_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  unpackaged_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)

DB side effects:
  - INSERT INTO cv_batches with status='germ', metrc_source_type='package', metrc_source_package_label=package_label, plant_count_initial=plant_count, plant_count_current=plant_count, sow_date=planted_date
  - If package_adjustment_amount is not null: UPDATE cv_metrc_packages SET weight_amount = weight_amount - package_adjustment_amount WHERE package_tag = package_label (if weight_amount exists) OR count = count - plant_count (if it's a count-based package)

Phase 5 API: POST /packages/v2/plantings — ASSUMED from MO docs, verify in MN docs.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #18: Plantings from Plant ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/plantings-from-plant.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/plantings-from-plant
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PlantingsFromPlants_template.csv
Exact headers: PlantLabel,PlantBatchName,PlantBatchType,PlantCount,StrainName,LocationName,SublocationName,PatientLicenseNumber,ActualDate

Request body:
  plant_label: string (24-char; mother plant tag from cv_metrc_plant_state)
  plant_batch_name: string
  plant_batch_type: string ('Clone' or 'Seed')
  plant_count: number (integer, positive)
  strain_name: string
  location_name: string (metrc_name from cv_locations)
  sublocation_name: string | null
  patient_license_number: string | null
  actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)

Validation: Verify plant_label exists in cv_metrc_plant_state and is_mother_plant=1. If not a mother plant, return 400 with error 'Plant [label] is not designated as a mother plant'.

DB side effects:
  - INSERT INTO cv_batches with status='germ', metrc_source_type='plant', metrc_source_plant_label=plant_label, plant_count_initial=plant_count, plant_count_current=plant_count, sow_date=actual_date
  - Mother plant cv_metrc_plant_state remains active (status stays 'active')

Phase 5 API: POST /plants/v2/plantings — ASSUMED from MO docs, verify in MN docs.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #22: Split Planting ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/split-planting.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/split-planting
Auth: requireAuth (supervisor+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/SplitPlanting_template.csv
Exact headers: PlantBatch,GroupName,Count,Location,Sublocation,Strain,PatientLicenseNumber,ActualDate

Note on headers: GroupName appears to be the new batch name created by the split.

Request body:
  plant_batch_id: number (ID of the cv_batches record being split)
  group_name: string (new batch name after split)
  count: number (integer, positive; plants moved into the new batch; must be <= source batch plant_count_current)
  location_name: string (metrc_name from cv_locations for new batch)
  sublocation_name: string | null
  patient_license_number: string | null
  actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)

Validation: Verify count <= source_batch.plant_count_current. If not, return 400.

CSV output: one row. PlantBatch = source batch metrc name (cv_batches.name or metrc_plant_batch_uid), GroupName = group_name, Count = count.

DB side effects:
  - UPDATE cv_batches SET plant_count_current = plant_count_current - count WHERE batch_id = plant_batch_id
  - INSERT INTO cv_batches for the new split batch: name=group_name, strain_id=(same as source), status='germ', plant_count_initial=count, plant_count_current=count, metrc_source_type='batch_split', metrc_source_batch_name=(source batch name), sow_date=actual_date

Phase 5 API: POST /plantbatches/v2/split — ASSUMED from MO docs, verify in MN docs.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npx tsc --noEmit passes
- No new migrations (all tables exist from migrations 042-044)
- Four new generator files created
- Four new route handlers added to metrc-csv.ts
- Each generator produces CSV with exact headers matching template files

Commit: feat(metrc-csv): implement planting family generators (#2, #17, #18, #22)
Push after commit.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
Four new route handlers added to src/api/routes/metrc-csv.ts

tasks.name: METRC CSV 3B: Destruction family #3 #4
tasks.instructions:
Read CLAUDE.md and docs/metrc-csv-design/integration-proposal.md before starting.

CONTEXT: Phase 3B of the METRC CSV Upload Service. Phase 1 infrastructure and Phase 3A planting family are already implemented. Study existing generator files in src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/ for the implementation pattern. The destruction family covers two types with similar structure but different semantics: immature batch destruction (count-based, no individual tags) vs. tagged plant destruction (individual 24-char METRC tags).

--- UPLOAD TYPE #3: Destroy Immature Plants ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/destroy-immature.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/destroy-immature
Auth: requireRole('supervisor') — destructive operation
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/DestroyImmaturePlants_template.csv
Exact headers: PlantBatch,Count,WasteMethodName,WasteMaterialMixed,WasteReasonName,ReasonNote,WasteWeight,WasteUnitOfMeasure,ActualDate

Note on template: WasteMethodName, WasteMaterialMixed, WasteWeight, WasteUnitOfMeasure are all NULLABLE (see sample row 3 in template which has empty method/material/weight). When waste_weight=0, leave WasteMethodName, WasteMaterialMixed, WasteUnitOfMeasure empty in the CSV.

Request body:
  plant_batch_id: number (cv_batches batch_id; must be in germ/seedling/cult-hoop status — not yet in field)
  count: number (integer, positive; plants to destroy; must be <= plant_count_current)
  waste_method_name: string | null (from cv_metrc_plant_waste_methods; null when waste_weight=0)
  waste_material_mixed: string | null
  waste_reason_name: string (from cv_metrc_batch_waste_reasons)
  reason_note: string | null
  waste_weight: number (0 = destruction with no waste weight recorded)
  waste_uom: string | null (from cv_metrc_units_of_measure; null when waste_weight=0)
  actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)

Lookup: Get batch from cv_batches. Use cv_batches.name (or a derived METRC name) for PlantBatch column. The METRC batch name should be stored in cv_batches — use the field that holds the METRC-facing batch name.

DB side effects:
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_immature_destruction_events (plant_batch_id, count, waste_method=waste_method_name, waste_material_mixed, waste_reason=waste_reason_name, reason_note, waste_weight, waste_uom, actual_date, created_by, created_at)
  - UPDATE cv_batches SET plant_count_current = plant_count_current - count WHERE batch_id = plant_batch_id
  - If new plant_count_current = 0: UPDATE cv_batches SET status = 'closed', closed_date = actual_date WHERE batch_id = plant_batch_id

Phase 5 API: DELETE /plantbatches/v2/ — CONFIRMED from user-provided API docs. METRC records destruction event in audit.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #4: Destroy Plants ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/destroy-plants.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/destroy-plants
Auth: requireRole('supervisor') — destructive operation
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/DestroyPlants_template.csv
Exact headers: Label,WasteMethodName,WasteMaterialMixed,WasteWeight,WasteUnitOfMeasureName,WasteReasonName,ReasonNote,ActualDate

Note: 'WasteUnitOfMeasureName' (not WasteUnitOfMeasure) — different from #3. Use the exact column name from the template.

Request body:
  plant_tags: string[] (array of 24-char METRC plant tags to destroy; each becomes one CSV row)
  waste_method_name: string (from cv_metrc_plant_waste_methods)
  waste_material_mixed: string | null
  waste_weight: number (per-plant weight; 0 allowed)
  waste_uom: string | null (null when waste_weight=0)
  waste_reason_name: string (from cv_metrc_plant_waste_reasons)
  reason_note: string | null
  actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)

Validation: All plant_tags must exist in cv_metrc_plant_state with status='active'. Return 400 with list of invalid tags if any are not found.

CSV output: one row per plant_tag. Label = the plant tag.

DB side effects (for EACH plant tag):
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_plant_state SET status='destroyed', metrc_csv_generated_at=now() WHERE plant_tag=tag
  - UPDATE cv_plant_assignments SET unassigned_at=actual_date, unassign_reason='destroyed', unassigned_by=req.user.id WHERE metrc_plant_tag=tag AND unassigned_at IS NULL
  - The plant_loss_events table METRC fields should be updated if a matching plant_loss_events record exists (join on metrc_plant_tag): SET metrc_csv_generated_at=now(), metrc_waste_method=waste_method_name, metrc_waste_reason=waste_reason_name, metrc_waste_weight=waste_weight, metrc_waste_uom=waste_uom WHERE metrc_plant_tag=tag AND metrc_sync_status='pending'
  - If no existing plant_loss_events record, insert one with these METRC fields populated and metrc_sync_status='synced'

Phase 5 API: DELETE /plants/v2/ — CONFIRMED from user-provided API docs. METRC records destruction event in audit.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npx tsc --noEmit passes
- No new migrations needed
- Two new generator files created
- Two new route handlers added to metrc-csv.ts
- Supervisor role enforced on both routes

Commit: feat(metrc-csv): implement destruction family generators (#3, #4)
Push after commit.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors

tasks.name: METRC CSV 3C: Immature batch #7 #8 #9
tasks.instructions:
Read CLAUDE.md and docs/metrc-csv-design/integration-proposal.md before starting.

CONTEXT: Phase 3C of the METRC CSV Upload Service. Implements three operations on immature batches (before individual plant tagging). Type #7 is the critical 'graduation' event that transitions a batch from immature to individually tagged Vegetative/Flowering plants — it creates cv_metrc_plant_state records and reserves tags from the pool.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #7: Immature Plants Growth Phase ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/immature-growth-phase.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/immature-growth-phase
Auth: requireRole('supervisor') — creates plant state records and consumes tag pool
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/ImmaturePlantsGrowthPhase_template.csv
Exact headers: Name,Count,StartingTag,GrowthPhase,NewLocation,NewSublocation,GrowthDate,PatientLicenseNumber

Note: ONE row per batch transition. StartingTag is the FIRST tag in the range — METRC assigns sequential tags from StartingTag to StartingTag+Count-1. The operator enters the starting tag; METRC allocates the range.

Request body:
  batch_id: number (cv_batches batch_id; must be in germ/seedling/cult-hoop status)
  count: number (integer, positive; must be <= plant_count_current of the batch)
  starting_tag: string (24-char; must exist in cv_metrc_available_plant_tags with status='available')
  growth_phase: string ('Vegetative' or 'Flowering' from cv_metrc_growth_phases)
  new_location: string (metrc_name from cv_locations)
  new_sublocation: string | null
  growth_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  patient_license_number: string | null

Tag range logic:
  - Query cv_metrc_available_plant_tags WHERE tag >= starting_tag ORDER BY tag LIMIT count
  - These must all have status='available' — if any are already used, return 400 with error
  - In the transaction: UPDATE cv_metrc_available_plant_tags SET status='used' for all tags in range

DB side effects (all in one transaction):
  - Validate that count tags starting from starting_tag are available in cv_metrc_available_plant_tags
  - For each tag in the range: INSERT INTO cv_metrc_plant_state (plant_tag=tag, plant_batch_id=batch_id, strain_id=batch.strain_id, growth_phase, location_id, phase_transition_date=growth_date, status='active', created_at, updated_at)
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_available_plant_tags SET status='used' for all tags in range
  - UPDATE cv_batches SET plant_count_current = plant_count_current - count WHERE batch_id = batch_id (plants have 'graduated' from the immature batch into individual tracked plants)
  - NOTE: Do NOT close the batch — remaining plants may still be in the immature batch

Phase 5 API: POST /plantbatches/v2/growthphase — ASSUMED from MO docs, verify in MN docs.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #8: Immature Plant Packages ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/immature-packages.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/immature-packages
Auth: requireRole('supervisor')
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/ImmaturePlantsPackages_template.csv
Exact headers: PlantBatch,Item,Tag,PatientLicenseNumber,Note,IsTradeSample,IsDonation,Count,Location,Sublocation,ActualDate

Note: IsTradeSample and IsDonation are boolean values. In the CSV, write 'True' or 'False' (title case, matching the template sample data).

Request body:
  plant_batch_id: number (cv_batches batch_id)
  item_name: string (from cv_metrc_items)
  package_tag: string (24-char; from cv_metrc_available_package_tags with status='available')
  patient_license_number: string | null
  note: string | null
  is_trade_sample: boolean (default false)
  is_donation: boolean (default false)
  count: number (integer, positive; plants to package; must be <= plant_count_current)
  location_name: string | null
  sublocation_name: string | null
  actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)

Lookup: Get batch name from cv_batches for PlantBatch column.

DB side effects:
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_packages (package_tag, item_name, source_type='immature_batch', source_plant_batch_id=plant_batch_id, count, location_id (lookup by location_name), is_trade_sample, is_donation, actual_date, created_by, created_at, updated_at)
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_available_package_tags SET status='used' WHERE tag=package_tag
  - UPDATE cv_batches SET plant_count_current = plant_count_current - count WHERE batch_id = plant_batch_id

Phase 5 API: POST /plantbatches/v2/packages — CONFIRMED from user-provided API docs. Returns Ids of created packages.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #9: Immature Plants Waste ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/immature-waste.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/immature-waste
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/ImmaturePlantsWaste_template.csv
Exact headers: WasteMethodName,MixedMaterial,WasteWeight,UnitOfMeasureName,ReasonName,Note,WasteDate,PlantBatchName

Note: This is the BATCH/end-of-day endpoint. The request body is an array of waste events.

Request body:
  events: array of {
    plant_batch_id: number
    waste_method_name: string
    mixed_material: string | null
    waste_weight: number (must be > 0)
    uom_name: string
    reason_name: string
    note: string | null
    waste_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  }

Lookup: For each event, get batch name from cv_batches for PlantBatchName column.

CSV output: one row per event in the array, max 500 rows total.

DB side effects (for each event):
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_immature_waste_events (plant_batch_id, waste_method=waste_method_name, mixed_material, waste_weight, waste_uom=uom_name, waste_reason=reason_name, note, waste_date, created_by, created_at)

Phase 5 API: POST /plantbatches/v2/waste — CONFIRMED from user-provided API docs.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npx tsc --noEmit passes
- No new migrations needed (cv_metrc_plant_state, cv_metrc_packages, cv_metrc_immature_waste_events all created in Phase 1 migrations)
- Three new generator files created
- Three new route handlers added to metrc-csv.ts
- Type #7 correctly updates cv_metrc_available_plant_tags to 'used' status

Commit: feat(metrc-csv): implement immature batch generators (#7, #8, #9)
Push after commit.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors

tasks.name: METRC CSV 3D: Plant lifecycle #19 #20
tasks.instructions:
Read CLAUDE.md and docs/metrc-csv-design/integration-proposal.md before starting.

CONTEXT: Phase 3D implements plant lifecycle operations on already-tagged individual plants (cv_metrc_plant_state records). Type #19 is the 'flip to flower' operation — it replaces each plant's tag with a new tag from the pool and changes the growth phase. Type #20 is a location-only move with no tag change.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #19: Plants Growth Phase ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/plants-growth-phase.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/plants-growth-phase
Auth: requireRole('supervisor') — consumes new tags from pool
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PlantsGrowthPhase_template.csv
Exact headers: Label,NewTag,GrowthPhase,NewLocation,NewSublocation,GrowthDate

Note: This is typically a batch operation affecting all plants in a sub-zone (e.g., 'flip to flower' = ~150 plants). The request accepts a list of (old_tag, new_tag) pairs. Each plant gets a NEW tag when it changes growth phase per METRC rules.

Request body:
  plants: array of {
    label: string (24-char; current plant tag from cv_metrc_plant_state)
    new_tag: string (24-char; must exist in cv_metrc_available_plant_tags with status='available')
    growth_phase: string ('Vegetative' or 'Flowering')
    new_location: string (metrc_name from cv_locations)
    new_sublocation: string | null
    growth_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  }
  max 500 plants per request (CSV 500-row limit)

Validation:
  - All label values must exist in cv_metrc_plant_state with status='active'
  - All new_tag values must exist in cv_metrc_available_plant_tags with status='available'
  - No duplicate new_tag values in the request
  - Return 400 with details if any validation fails

DB side effects (all in one transaction, for each plant entry):
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_plant_state SET plant_tag=new_tag, previous_plant_tag=label, tag_change_date=growth_date, growth_phase=growth_phase, location_id=(lookup cv_locations by metrc_name=new_location), phase_transition_date=growth_date, updated_at=now() WHERE plant_tag=label
  - UPDATE cv_plant_assignments SET metrc_plant_tag=new_tag WHERE metrc_plant_tag=label AND unassigned_at IS NULL (update the active assignment to reflect the new tag)
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_available_plant_tags SET status='used' WHERE tag=new_tag
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_available_plant_tags SET status='replaced' WHERE tag=label (the old tag is now retired — use a distinct status to distinguish from 'used')

Response: Include tags_consumed list of all new tags used.

Phase 5 API: PUT /plants/v2/growthphase — CONFIRMED from user-provided API docs. No response body on success.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #20: Plants Location ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/plants-location.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/plants-location
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PlantsLocation_template.csv
Exact headers: Label,Location,Sublocation,ActualDate

Note: Location-only move, no tag change. Simpler than #19.

Request body:
  plants: array of {
    label: string (24-char; current plant tag)
    location: string (metrc_name from cv_locations)
    sublocation: string | null
    actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  }
  max 500 plants per request

Validation: All label values must exist in cv_metrc_plant_state with status='active'.

DB side effects (for each plant):
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_plant_state SET location_id=(lookup cv_locations by metrc_name=location), sublocation_id=(lookup cv_metrc_sublocations or store sublocation text), updated_at=now() WHERE plant_tag=label
  - Note: sublocation may be a free-text string if cv_metrc_sublocations table is not yet populated. Store the sublocation string in a TEXT column on cv_metrc_plant_state if one exists, or skip the sublocation update if no column exists yet.

Phase 5 API: PUT /plants/v2/location — CONFIRMED from user-provided API docs. No response body on success.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npx tsc --noEmit passes
- Type #19 correctly marks old tags as 'replaced' and new tags as 'used' in cv_metrc_available_plant_tags
- Type #19 updates cv_plant_assignments.metrc_plant_tag for all active assignments
- Two new generator files and two new route handlers added

Commit: feat(metrc-csv): implement plant lifecycle generators (#19, #20)
Push after commit.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors

tasks.name: METRC CSV 3E: Harvest family #5 #11 #15
tasks.instructions:
Read CLAUDE.md and docs/metrc-csv-design/integration-proposal.md before starting.

CONTEXT: Phase 3E implements harvest-related operations. All three types interact with Cultivate's existing harvest tables (cv_harvest_batches, cv_plant_harvest_events). Review docs/harvest-model.md for the harvest model details. Types #5 and #11 are the primary harvest events; #15 is post-harvest packaging.

IMPORTANT: Cultivate uses 'final_harvest' for what METRC calls full harvest (#5) and 'partial_harvest' for what METRC calls 'manicure' (#11). The CSV file is ManicurePlants.csv for #11. Do NOT use the word 'manicure' in the UI or user-facing strings, only in internal file names/endpoints where required.

IMPORTANT: HarvestName must always be provided by Cultivate (never left blank) to avoid METRC auto-generation which can't be captured (PUT /plants/v2/harvest returns no response body). Derive harvest names from cv_harvest_batches.harvest_name or generate one as '{strain}_{sow_date}_batch{sequence}'.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #5: Harvest Plants ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/harvest-plants.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/harvest-plants
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/HarvestPlants_template.csv
Exact headers: Plant,Weight,UnitOfWeight,DryingLocation,DryingSublocation,HarvestName,PatientLicenseNumber,ActualDate

Request body:
  harvest_batch_id: number (cv_harvest_batches harvest_batch_id)
  plant_events: array of {
    plant_tag: string (24-char; from cv_metrc_plant_state)
    weight: number (wet weight)
    unit_of_weight: string (from cv_metrc_units_of_measure)
    drying_location: string (metrc_name from cv_locations)
    drying_sublocation: string | null
    actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
    patient_license_number: string | null
  }

Lookup: Get cv_harvest_batches by harvest_batch_id to get harvest_name for HarvestName column. All events share the same harvest batch.

DB side effects (for each plant_event):
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_plant_state SET status='harvested', updated_at=now() WHERE plant_tag=plant_tag
  - UPDATE cv_plant_assignments SET unassigned_at=actual_date, unassign_reason='harvested', unassigned_by=req.user.id WHERE metrc_plant_tag=plant_tag AND unassigned_at IS NULL
  - INSERT INTO cv_plant_harvest_events (harvest_batch_id, plant_batch_id=batch.plant_batch_id, plant_assignment_id=(get from assignment), container_id=(get from assignment), event_type='final_harvest', harvested_at=actual_date, wet_weight=weight, weight_unit=unit_of_weight, applicator=req.user.id, metrc_csv_generated_at=now())
    OR if a cv_plant_harvest_events row already exists for this plant_tag and this harvest_batch_id, update it with metrc_csv_generated_at=now()
  - After all events: Check if all plants in harvest_batch_id's plant_batch have final_harvest events. If so, UPDATE cv_batches SET status='closed', closed_date=actual_date.

Phase 5 API: PUT /plants/v2/harvest — CONFIRMED. Returns NO response body on success (cannot capture METRC harvest name from response — hence always provide HarvestName).

--- UPLOAD TYPE #11: Manicure Plants ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/manicure-plants.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/manicure-plants
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/ManicurePlants_template.csv
Exact headers: Plant,Weight,UnitOfWeight,DryingLocation,DryingSublocation,HarvestName,PatientLicenseNumber,ActualDate,PlantCount

Note: PlantCount column is the last column. The integration proposal notes Q42 semantics are unclear — implement it as an optional field, defaulting to 1 if not provided. Plant remains ALIVE after manicure.

Request body:
  harvest_batch_id: number (cv_harvest_batches harvest_batch_id)
  plant_events: array of {
    plant_tag: string (24-char)
    weight: number
    unit_of_weight: string
    drying_location: string
    drying_sublocation: string | null
    actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
    patient_license_number: string | null
    plant_count: number | null (default 1 if null)
  }

DB side effects (for each plant_event):
  - cv_metrc_plant_state.status remains 'active' (plant survives)
  - INSERT INTO cv_plant_harvest_events (harvest_batch_id, plant_batch_id=batch.plant_batch_id, plant_assignment_id, container_id, event_type='partial_harvest', harvested_at=actual_date, wet_weight=weight, weight_unit=unit_of_weight, plant_count=plant_count, applicator=req.user.id, metrc_csv_generated_at=now())
  - UPDATE cv_plant_harvest_events SET metrc_event_id=NULL for now (Phase 5 will backfill from POST /plants/v2/manicure response which returns Ids)

Phase 5 API: POST /plants/v2/manicure — CONFIRMED. Returns Ids of created harvest event records.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #15: Packages From Harvest ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/packages-from-harvest.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/packages-from-harvest
Auth: requireRole('supervisor')
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PackagesFromHarvest_template.csv
Exact headers: Tag,Location,Sublocation,Item,UnitOfWeight,PatientLicenseNumber,Note,ProductionBatchNumber,IsTradeSample,IsDonation,ActualDate,IngredientHarvestName,IngredientWeight,IngredientUnitOfWeight

NOTE ON CSV STRUCTURE: The sample data shows that each row represents ONE package with ONE ingredient harvest. If a package is sourced from multiple harvests, the current template structure suggests one row per ingredient (same Tag repeated). This is the same denormalization pattern as the Additive Template (#1). Study the additive-template generator for how to handle one logical entity producing multiple CSV rows.

Request body:
  packages: array of {
    tag: string (24-char; from cv_metrc_available_package_tags)
    location_name: string | null
    sublocation_name: string | null
    item_name: string (from cv_metrc_items)
    unit_of_weight: string
    patient_license_number: string | null
    note: string | null
    production_batch_number: string | null
    is_trade_sample: boolean (default false)
    is_donation: boolean (default false)
    actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
    ingredients: array of {
      harvest_name: string (must match cv_harvest_batches.harvest_name or metrc_harvest_batch_uid)
      weight: number
      unit_of_weight: string
    }
  }

CSV output: one row per (package, ingredient) combination. IsTradeSample/IsDonation written as 'True'/'False'.

DB side effects (for each package):
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_packages (package_tag=tag, item_name, source_type='harvest', source_harvest_ingredients=JSON(ingredients), is_trade_sample, is_donation, actual_date, created_by, created_at, updated_at)
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_available_package_tags SET status='used' WHERE tag=tag
  - For each ingredient: find cv_harvest_batches WHERE harvest_name matches or metrc_harvest_batch_uid matches, then UPDATE cv_harvest_batches SET total_packaged_weight = total_packaged_weight + weight

Phase 5 API: POST /harvests/v2/packages — ASSUMED from MO docs, verify in MN docs.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npx tsc --noEmit passes
- Three new generator files and three route handlers added
- Type #5 auto-closes batch when all plants are final-harvested
- Type #11 leaves plant status='active'
- Type #15 writes multiple rows per package when ingredients > 1

Commit: feat(metrc-csv): implement harvest family generators (#5, #11, #15)
Push after commit.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors

tasks.name: METRC CSV 3F: Additive apps #6 #10 #16
tasks.instructions:
Read CLAUDE.md and docs/metrc-csv-design/integration-proposal.md before starting.

CONTEXT: Phase 3F implements the three additive application upload types. These are all CSV-ONLY — there is no METRC API endpoint for them in MN. The response must always include metrc_submission: { status: 'csv_only' }. All three generate CSVs linking additive template applications to their targets (immature batch, individual plants, or location).

All three are BATCH/end-of-day endpoints — the operator accumulates applications throughout the day and submits them together.

The cv_metrc_additive_applications table (created in migration 042) stores all three types differentiated by application_type field.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #6: Immature Plant Additives ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/immature-additive-apps.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/additive-applications/immature-batch
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/ImmaturePlantsAdditivesUsingTemplate_template.csv
Exact headers: PlantBatchName,AdditivesTemplateName,Rate,Volume,TotalAmountApplied,TotalAmountUnitOfMeasure,ActualDate
Date format: From sample data — 12/15/2019 12:00:00 AM (M/D/YYYY h:mm:ss tt format, NOT YYYY-MM-DD). Use this exact format when writing ActualDate to the CSV.

Request body:
  applications: array of {
    plant_batch_id: number (cv_batches batch_id; must be in immature status germ/seedling/cult-hoop)
    template_name: string (must match cv_metrc_additive_templates.name)
    rate: string | null (opaque string e.g. '5 Pounds')
    volume: string | null (opaque string e.g. '1000 Sq. ft.')
    total_amount_applied: number
    total_amount_uom: string
    actual_date: string (ISO datetime or YYYY-MM-DD; convert to M/D/YYYY h:mm:ss AM format for CSV)
  }

Lookup: For each application, get cv_batches.name for PlantBatchName column.

DB side effects (for each application):
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_additive_applications (application_type='immature_batch', template_id=(lookup by name), target_plant_batch_id=plant_batch_id, rate, volume, total_amount_applied, total_amount_uom, actual_date, created_by, created_at)

Phase 5: CSV-ONLY — no API submission. Always return { metrc_submission: { status: 'csv_only' } }.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #10: Location Additives ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/location-additive-apps.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/additive-applications/location
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/LocationAdditivesUsingTemplate_template.csv
Exact headers: LocationName,SublocationName,AdditivesTemplateName,Rate,Volume,TotalAmountApplied,TotalAmountUnitOfMeasure,ActualDate
Date format: Same M/D/YYYY h:mm:ss AM format as #6.

Request body:
  applications: array of {
    location_name: string (metrc_name from cv_locations)
    sublocation_name: string | null
    template_name: string (cv_metrc_additive_templates.name)
    rate: string | null
    volume: string | null
    total_amount_applied: number
    total_amount_uom: string
    actual_date: string
  }

DB side effects (for each):
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_additive_applications (application_type='location', template_id, target_location_id=(lookup cv_locations by metrc_name), target_sublocation=sublocation_name, rate, volume, total_amount_applied, total_amount_uom, actual_date, created_by, created_at)

Phase 5: CSV-ONLY.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #16: Plant Additives ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/plant-additive-apps.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/additive-applications/plants
Auth: requireAuth (grover+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PlantAdditvesUsingTemplate_template.csv
(NOTE: The template filename has a typo 'Additves' — use the exact filename as found on disk)
Exact headers: PlantTag,AdditivesTemplateName,Rate,Volume,TotalAmountApplied,TotalAmountUnitOfMeasure,ActualDate
Date format: Same M/D/YYYY h:mm:ss AM format.

Request body:
  applications: array of {
    plant_tag: string (24-char; must exist in cv_metrc_plant_state with status='active')
    template_name: string
    rate: string | null
    volume: string | null
    total_amount_applied: number
    total_amount_uom: string
    actual_date: string
  }

DB side effects (for each):
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_additive_applications (application_type='plant', template_id, target_plant_tag=plant_tag, rate, volume, total_amount_applied, total_amount_uom, actual_date, created_by, created_at)

Phase 5: CSV-ONLY.

SHARED IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: Create a helper function formatMetrcDatetime(isoDate: string): string that converts ISO dates (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss) to the M/D/YYYY h:mm:ss tt format used in additive application CSVs. Put it in src/lib/metrc-csv/types.ts or a new date-utils.ts helper.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npx tsc --noEmit passes
- All three generators produce CSV-ONLY response (no API submission path)
- Date format in CSV matches M/D/YYYY h:mm:ss AM template exactly
- Three generator files and three route handlers added

Commit: feat(metrc-csv): implement additive application generators (#6, #10, #16)
Push after commit.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
All three routes return metrc_submission: { status: 'csv_only' }

tasks.name: METRC CSV 3G: Packages #12 #13 #14
tasks.instructions:
Read CLAUDE.md and docs/metrc-csv-design/integration-proposal.md before starting.

CONTEXT: Phase 3G implements three package-related upload types. Type #12 (Package Adjustment) is a relatively straightforward adjustment to an existing package. Type #13 (Package from Veg Plants) uses the Plant Group Tagging feature — the API endpoint is unknown for MN; implement CSV generation fully but mark Phase 5 submission as TBD. Type #14 (Package Planting from Plant) creates both a package AND a new batch from a mother plant.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #12: Package Adjustment ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/package-adjustment.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/package-adjustment
Auth: requireAuth (grower+)
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PackageAdjustment_template.csv
Exact headers: Label,Quantity,UnitOfMeasure,AdjustmentReason,ReasonNote,AdjustmentDate,EmployeeLicenseNumber

Note on Quantity: This is a SIGNED decimal per the integration proposal (positive = increase, negative = decrease). The spec notes a 'signed_decimal' data type.

Request body:
  package_tag: string (24-char; must exist in cv_metrc_packages)
  quantity: number (signed; non-zero; negative = decrease, positive = increase)
  unit_of_measure: string (from cv_metrc_units_of_measure)
  adjustment_reason: string (from cv_metrc_package_adjustment_reasons)
  reason_note: string | null
  adjustment_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  employee_id: number (cv_employees.employee_id; provides the license number)

Lookup: Get cv_employees.license_number for EmployeeLicenseNumber column.

DB side effects:
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_package_adjustments (package_id=(lookup by package_tag), quantity_change=quantity, unit_of_measure, adjustment_reason, reason_note, adjustment_date, employee_id, created_by, created_at)
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_packages SET weight_amount = weight_amount + quantity WHERE package_tag=package_tag (if weight_amount column exists; otherwise update count or note that weight tracking may not apply)

Phase 5 API: POST /packages/v2/adjust — CONFIRMED. Returns { Ids: [...], Warnings } for new adjustments. NOTE: PUT /packages/v2/adjust also exists (for updating existing adjustments, returns no body). For new adjustments from CSV use POST.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #13: Package from Vegetative Plants ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/package-from-veg.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/package-from-veg
Auth: requireRole('supervisor')
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PackageFromVegetativePlants_template.csv
Exact headers: Label,Location,Sublocation,Item,ActualDate,Note,IsTradeSample,IsDonation,ExpirationDate,SellByDate,UseByDate,PlantGroupLabel,Quantity

Note: PlantGroupLabel is a METRC Plant Group Tagging feature — the operator enters the plant group label that already exists in METRC. Cultivate does not manage plant groups natively. IsTradeSample/IsDonation are written as 'True'/'False'. ExpirationDate, SellByDate, UseByDate are optional (empty if null).

Request body:
  package_tag: string (24-char; from cv_metrc_available_package_tags)
  location_name: string | null
  sublocation_name: string | null
  item_name: string (from cv_metrc_items)
  actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)
  note: string | null
  is_trade_sample: boolean (default false)
  is_donation: boolean (default false)
  expiration_date: string | null (YYYY-MM-DD)
  sell_by_date: string | null (YYYY-MM-DD)
  use_by_date: string | null (YYYY-MM-DD)
  plant_group_label: string (24-char; the plant group tag from METRC — NOT validated against Cultivate DB since plant groups are not tracked here)
  quantity: number | null

DB side effects:
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_packages (package_tag, item_name, source_type='plant_group', source_plant_group_label=plant_group_label, location_id, is_trade_sample, is_donation, actual_date, expiration_date, sell_by_date, use_by_date, created_by, created_at, updated_at)
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_available_package_tags SET status='used' WHERE tag=package_tag

Phase 5 API: UNKNOWN — MN API endpoint for plant group packaging is not documented. Set metrc_submission: { status: 'api_unknown', note: 'Package from Vegetative Plants API endpoint not confirmed for MN. CSV generated; manual upload required.' } in response.

--- UPLOAD TYPE #14: Package Planting from Plant ---
File: src/lib/metrc-csv/generators/package-planting-from-plant.ts
Route: POST /api/metrc/csv/package-planting-from-plant
Auth: requireRole('supervisor')
CSV template: docs/metrc-csv-design/PackagePlantingFromPlant_template.csv
Exact headers: PlantLabel,PackageTag,PlantBatchType,Item,Location,Sublocation,Note,PatientLicenseNumber,IsTradeSample,IsDonation,Count,ActualDate

Note: This creates BOTH a package AND a new plant batch from a mother plant. The plant label identifies the mother plant (cv_metrc_plant_state.is_mother_plant=1). Mother plant stays active.

Request body:
  plant_label: string (24-char; mother plant tag; must exist in cv_metrc_plant_state with is_mother_plant=1)
  package_tag: string (24-char; from cv_metrc_available_package_tags)
  plant_batch_type: string ('Clone' or 'Seed')
  item_name: string (from cv_metrc_items)
  location_name: string | null
  sublocation_name: string | null
  note: string | null
  patient_license_number: string | null
  is_trade_sample: boolean (default false)
  is_donation: boolean (default false)
  count: number (integer, positive; number of plants in the package)
  actual_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD)

Validation: Verify plant_label exists in cv_metrc_plant_state with is_mother_plant=1.

DB side effects:
  - INSERT INTO cv_metrc_packages (package_tag, item_name, source_type='mother_plant', source_plant_label=plant_label, plant_batch_type, count, location_id, is_trade_sample, is_donation, actual_date, created_by, created_at, updated_at)
  - UPDATE cv_metrc_available_package_tags SET status='used' WHERE tag=package_tag
  - INSERT INTO cv_batches for the new batch created inside the package: status='germ', metrc_source_type='plant', metrc_source_plant_label=plant_label, plant_count_initial=count, plant_count_current=count, sow_date=actual_date
  - Mother plant cv_metrc_plant_state.status remains 'active'

Phase 5 API: POST /plantbatches/v2/packages/frommotherplant — CONFIRMED from user-provided API docs. Despite the name, creates a package containing a new batch from the mother plant.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npx tsc --noEmit passes
- Type #13 returns api_unknown status in response
- Type #14 creates both a package AND a new cv_batches row in one transaction
- Three generator files and three route handlers added

Commit: feat(metrc-csv): implement package generators (#12, #13, #14)
Push after commit.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
Type #14 creates both cv_metrc_packages and cv_batches rows in one transaction

tasks.name: METRC CSV Phase 4: Validation Engine
tasks.instructions:
Read CLAUDE.md and docs/metrc-csv-design/integration-proposal.md before starting.

CONTEXT: Phase 4 implements the validation engine for the METRC CSV Upload Service. All 22 generator files now exist. The goal is to centralize validation logic, add cross-field and cross-record rules, and add comprehensive integration tests.

The validation is split into three layers:
1. INPUT VALIDATION: Zod schemas in route handlers (mostly already inline; move to src/lib/metrc-csv/validators/)
2. BUSINESS RULE VALIDATION: Cross-field rules in generator functions
3. CROSS-RECORD VALIDATION: Rules that query the DB to validate inter-record constraints

FILE STRUCTURE to create:
  src/lib/metrc-csv/validators/
    index.ts         — exports all validators
    shared.ts        — shared validators (METRC tag format, UoM lookup, etc.)
    plantings.ts     — validators for #2, #17, #18, #22
    destruction.ts   — validators for #3, #4
    immature.ts      — validators for #7, #8, #9
    plant-lifecycle.ts — validators for #19, #20
    harvest.ts       — validators for #5, #11, #15
    additives.ts     — validators for #1, #6, #10, #16
    packages.ts      — validators for #12, #13, #14
    csv-rules.ts     — universal CSV rules (500-row limit, header validation, encoding)

VALIDATION RULES TO IMPLEMENT:

--- Universal CSV Rules (csv-rules.ts) ---
  - MAX_ROWS = 500: If any generator produces > 500 rows, throw CsvTooLargeError with count and type
  - Header validation: Each generator's output headers must exactly match the confirmed template headers (hardcode expected headers per type; validate at write time in the CsvWriter)
  - CSV-safe strings: Detect and escape values containing commas, double-quotes, or CRLF by wrapping in double-quotes and doubling internal quotes per RFC 4180
  - Encoding: Ensure no BOM, CRLF line endings — already in writer.ts; add assertion tests

--- Shared Rules (shared.ts) ---
  - validateMetrcTag(tag: string): asserts 24 alphanumeric chars. On fail: throw MetrcTagFormatError.
  - validateTagAvailable(db, tag: string): queries cv_metrc_available_plant_tags or cv_metrc_available_package_tags; throws if status != 'available'
  - validateBatchExists(db, batch_id: number): queries cv_batches; throws 404 if not found
  - validateBatchStatus(batch, allowed: string[]): throws if batch.status not in allowed
  - validatePlantStateActive(db, tag: string): queries cv_metrc_plant_state; throws if not found or status != 'active'
  - validateUomExists(db, uom: string): queries cv_metrc_units_of_measure; throws if not found
  - validateTemplateExists(db, name: string): queries cv_metrc_additive_templates; throws if not found
  - validateLocationExists(db, metrc_name: string): queries cv_locations; throws if not found

--- Destruction-specific Rules (destruction.ts) ---
  - Waste method required when waste_weight > 0 (cross-field)
  - Waste UoM required when waste_weight > 0 (cross-field)
  - count must be <= batch.plant_count_current (#3)
  - Produce a WARNING (not error) when waste_weight = 0 and waste_method is null: 'No waste weight or method provided — recording zero-waste destruction'

--- Immature Growth Phase Rules (immature.ts) ---
  - Count tag availability: Query cv_metrc_available_plant_tags ORDER BY tag LIMIT count starting from starting_tag; all must have status='available'. Collect any used/replaced tags as errors.
  - starting_tag format must be valid 24-char alphanumeric

--- Plant Growth Phase Rules (plant-lifecycle.ts) ---
  - No duplicate new_tag values within a single request (#19)
  - No overlap between label (old tags) and new_tag values in the same request
  - Batch all validations before transaction; return all errors at once rather than failing on first

--- Harvest Rules (harvest.ts) ---
  - Batch status must be 'harvesting' to record final_harvest or partial_harvest events (#5, #11)
  - HarvestName must not be empty — error if blank
  - weight must be > 0

--- Package Rules (packages.ts) ---
  - Package tag must exist in cv_metrc_available_package_tags (#8, #13, #14, #15)
  - Package adjustment quantity must be non-zero (#12)
  - Quantity signed decimal: positive or negative, never 0 (#12)

--- Warning vs Error Policy ---
  Errors block generation (throw, return 400)
  Warnings allow generation but are included in response warnings array
  Warning examples:
    - METRC sublocation not found in cv_metrc_sublocations (use as-is, may cause METRC rejection)
    - UoM not in cv_metrc_units_of_measure (pass-through, METRC will validate)
    - Batch count will reach zero after this operation (informational)
    - Patient license number provided for non-medical state (may be ignored by METRC)

INTEGRATION TESTS to write in src/tests/metrc-csv.test.ts:
  - 500-row limit: Generate 501 events for plants-waste route; expect 400 with row count error
  - Header validation: Mock a generator that returns wrong headers; expect CsvHeaderMismatchError
  - METRC tag format: Call any route with a 23-char tag; expect 400 with format error
  - Tag availability: Call plants-growth-phase with a tag that has status='used'; expect 400
  - Batch status gate: Call harvest-plants when batch.status='field-flower'; expect 400
  - Zero waste destruction: Call destroy-immature with waste_weight=0; expect 200 with warning
  - CSV-safe escaping: Generate a row with a comma in a field value; CSV output must quote the field
  - CRLF line endings: Read generated CSV file bytes; assert CRLF (0x0D 0x0A) between rows, no BOM

EXPORT VALIDATORS from index.ts:
  All validator functions should be callable from generator files. Refactor existing generators to use the shared validators instead of inline checks (where they exist — don't duplicate validation).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npx tsc --noEmit passes
- src/lib/metrc-csv/validators/ directory exists with all 8 files
- All tests in src/tests/metrc-csv.test.ts pass
- 500-row limit is enforced in CsvWriter or generators
- CSV-safe string escaping is implemented and tested

Commit: feat(metrc-csv): implement Phase 4 validation engine and integration tests
Push after commit.
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
src/tests/metrc-csv.test.ts exists with at least 8 test cases
CRLF line endings verified in tests
